duration_note: Duration estimated from request-to-result timestamp gaps. 21 tasks had realistic (<30min) execution times avg 846s. Remaining include queue/overnight wait.

failure_patterns.description: SSE stream parsed but UI showed error. DuckDuckGo uses non-standard SSE format that APF block response disrupts.
HPACK dynamic table corruption when injecting block response into HTTP/2 stream. Fixed in later tasks via HPACK reset approach.
Block response delivered at transport layer but UI did not render warning. Frontend JS framework consumed response but showed generic error instead of warning.
Mistral SSE format uses custom event types. Block injection partially works but stream termination inconsistent, causing partial rendering.
JSON API response blocked but frontend showed generic error page instead of warning message. Block response content-type or structure mismatch.

bottleneck_observations: DuckDuckGo has highest task count (17) but lowest success rate (29%) - non-standard SSE and HTTP/2 behavior makes it the hardest service to block cleanly.
Qianwen has 12 tasks but only 17% classified as success - many results lack clear status fields (UNKNOWN), suggesting result format inconsistency rather than actual failures.
32 tasks (32%) have 'unknown' service classification - filename conventions were inconsistent for batch/multi-service tasks.
43 tasks (43%) have UNKNOWN status - result JSON schema varied significantly across sessions, making automated success/fail classification unreliable.
Top 5 slowest tasks all >15 minutes - DuckDuckGo and multi-service batch tasks dominate, suggesting complex browser automation sequences are the primary time sink.
No individual task duration tracking in result JSON - only single timestamp recorded, making precise execution time measurement impossible without git commit history analysis.
test_pc_experience_notes: Desktop Commander read_file returns metadata-only for JSON files - must use PowerShell Get-Content as fallback.
DPI scaling (125%, 1536x864 logical) causes coordinate mismatches in browser automation - must account for scale factor.
mcp__windows-mcp__PowerShell runs as non-admin (AzureAD user) - certificate store operations silently fail without error.
Git operations require .cmd file pattern with ASCII-only text and chcp 65001 for reliable execution.
CA certificate was deleted during testing (#464-465) - HTTPS tests through VT MITM will fail until reinstalled.
Browser automation for SSE-streaming services is fragile - page state changes mid-verification causing stale element errors.
write_file 25-30 line chunk limit requires multi-part writes for any non-trivial result JSON.
recommendations: Standardize result JSON schema: always include overall_status, started_at, completed_at, duration_seconds fields.
Add service name as explicit field in both request and result JSON instead of relying on filename parsing.
Prioritize DuckDuckGo and Mistral warning implementation - highest failure rates indicate block mechanism needs redesign for these services.
Implement structured error taxonomy in results to enable automated failure classification.
Consider splitting batch tasks into single-service requests for clearer metrics tracking.
